channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Six former Florida State players suing coach Leonard Hamilton over failed NIL payments
item.description: Six former Florida State basketball players are suing Seminoles coach Leonard Hamilton and alleging he failed to make good on a promise to get each of them $250,000 in name, image and likeness compensation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Will Ferrell appears at Kings game as disheveled Buddy the Elf
item.description: Actor Will Ferrell showed up to the Crypto.com Arena on Sunday dressed as a disheveled Buddy the Elf. He watched the Los Angeles Kings defeat the Philadelphia Flyers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bay Area sports rewind: Recounting the wild ride that was 2024
item.description: From the 49ers' Super Bowl heartbreak to San Jose State's volleyball firestorm to the death of baseball icons and the departure of the A's, the last 12 months have been eventful for Bay Area fans.
item.dc:creator: Evan Webeck, The Mercury News

item.title: Inside the Texans: Playoff-bound Texans reflect on losses, prepare for Titans
item.description: The Houston Texans are trying to move ahead on a short week after losing one of their most productive offensive weapons to a knee injury during Saturday’s 27-19 loss to the Kansas City Chiefs.
item.dc:creator: KPRC 2

item.title: JuJu Watkins scores 31 points to propel No. 4 Southern California past No. 23 Michigan 78-58
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — JuJu Watkins scored 31 points, making 13 of 14 free throws, and No. 4 Southern California beat No. 23 Michigan 78-58 on Sunday night in the Trojans’ Big Ten home opener. Watkins had five rebounds, seven assists, three steals and three blocks.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LeBron James at 40: A milestone birthday arrives Monday for the NBA's all-time scoring leader
item.description: When LeBron James broke another NBA record earlier this month, the one for most regular-season minutes played in a career, his Los Angeles Lakers teammates handled the moment in typical locker room fashion. They made fun of him. “They told me I’m old as hell,” James quipped.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: All smiles as Djokovic and Kyrgios treat crowd to flashy shots in doubles win
item.description: Exchanging smiles, laughs and flashy shots, Novak Djokovic and Nick Kyrgios have teamed up in doubles as they both returned to the court in preparation for the Australian Open.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Chip Kelly returns to the Rose Bowl, this time as Ohio State's offensive coordinator
item.description: Of all the things Chip Kelly is looking forward to in his return trip to Southern California, there is one that rises above everything else. “Gotta get more sunscreen looking at the weather report, because we haven’t seen the sun for a little while in Columbus," said Kelly with a wry smile.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Falcons run out of time to control their path to the playoffs after mismanaging the clock
item.description: The Atlanta Falcons have run out of time to control their path to the playoffs after mismanaging the clock multiple times in an overtime loss at the Washington Commanders.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jayden Daniels dazzles again as Commanders clinch a playoff spot by beating Falcons 30-24 in OT
item.description: LANDOVER, Md. (AP) — Jayden Daniels felt tired. Tired from a game that went well past 60 minutes. Tired from running all over the field for the better part of three-plus hours. It was all worth it after carrying the Washington Commanders into the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bills clinch the AFC's No. 2 seed with a 40-14 rout of the undisciplined Jets
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Josh Allen reflected on Buffalo’s many offseason critics and doubters when asked about the Bills achieving their latest milestones while sewing up the AFC’s No. 2 seed with a 40-14 rout of the unraveling and undisciplined New York Jets on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bryson Daily leads No. 19 Army to 27-6 victory over Louisiana Tech in the Independence Bowl
item.description: SHREVEPORT, La. (AP) — Bryson Daily broke the FBS record for touchdowns by a quarterback in a season with 32, running for three scores in No. 19 Army’s 27-6 victory over Louisiana Tech in the Independence Bowl on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: ROY LANG III, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Giddey's triple-double sends Bulls to 116-111 win over Bucks
item.description: Josh Giddey had 23 points, 15 rebounds and 10 assists for his second triple-double this season to lead the Chicago Bulls to a 116-111 win over the Milwaukee Bucks on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Athletics president Dave Kaval will resign from organization in the coming days
item.description: (AP) -- Athletics president Dave Kaval will resign from the organization after being the public face of the organization's planned move from Oakland to Las Vegas. Kaval has been president of the A's for the past eight years and will step down from the role on Dec.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Cathedral Catholic goalie shows why she's a keeper; honors her coach through art
item.description: Cathedral Catholic's 4-year starting goalie Maria'O Brien expresses her love to her head soccer coach through art. Suprising and touching longtime Cathedral Coach Dawn Lee.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Holiday Bowl to kick off at brand-new Snapdragon Stadium for the first time
item.description: The Holiday Bowl has been woven into the fabric of San Diego for four decades. For a football city without a professional sports team, there is even more excitement surrounding this annual event.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Reports: Pete Carroll interested in Bears' head-coaching job
item.description: Former Seattle Seahawks head coach Pete Carroll has expressed interest in becoming the next head coach of the Chicago Bears, who are looking to fill their head coaching vacancy for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: USF beats SJSU 41-39 in the Hawaii Bowl when Mac Harris knocks down a pass in the 5th overtime
item.description: Bryce Archie found Keshaun Singleton over the middle to give South Florida the lead in the fifth overtime and Mac Harris batted down a pass in the end zone to give the Bulls a 41-39 victory over San Jose State on Tuesday night in the Hawaii Bowl.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: BYU arrives in San Antonio ahead of Alamo Bowl
item.description: SAN ANTONIO, Tex. (ABC4 Sports) - The Cougars have arrived in San Antonio for what they hope will be an Alamo Bowl to remember. "Super excited to be down here," said defensive end Tyler Batty.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jazz winning streak ends at Cleveland, 124-113
item.description: CLEVELAND (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz first winning streak of the season did not last very long. Darius Garland scored 23 points and the NBA-best Cleveland Cavaliers beat the Utah Jazz 124-113 on Monday night for their 17th victory in 18 home games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Winter Classic Recap: Six local teams finish 2-1
item.description: Now that school is out for Christmas break and high school basketball is on a hiatus until early 2025, let's recap a great weekend of hoops across the Grand Valley.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
